# Parameters

config

: Configuration object

password

: Password for obtaining access token

totp

: Time-based one-time password

verbose

: Log requests/responses

use_keychain

: Store credentials securely in system keychain

store_token

: Store access token

login

: Login to API

mirror

: Directory to use for caching mirrored responses (created if not existing)

mirror_mode
: Mode of response mirroring:
  : - `dump`: Save responses in mirror cache
    - `load`: Load responses from mirror cache (if available)
    - `both`: Both dump and load
    - `none`: Do not dump or load

\_adapter

: Optional requests adapter instance (e.g. {obj}`requests.adapters.HTTPAdapter`).
  Useful for testing.

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.domain.TimeseriesProjection.list_timeseries_data
documentation:
List Timeseries Data **Pagination Sortable Columns:** `resource`, `data_type`,\`last_timestamp\`,\`created\`, `updated` **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.storage.read`. `listTimeseriesData`: `POST` `/api/v4/timeseries/list` # Parameters pagination_type : Method of handling pages in the returned data. There are three options available;\

limits: This is used with the query parameters `page` to select which page of data to return. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data.\

cursor: This is used with the query parameters `next` and `previous` which uses a string as the start or end location reference. The string to use is returned in the previous query json response in the keys `next_page` and `previous_page`.\

stream: This will return all the data in one response. (limits, cursor, stream) page_size : Number of items to be returned in each page. Page size can be between 1 and 1000 objects.\

The default is 20 page : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `limits`.\

An integer for the wanted page of results. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data. next : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the next page in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key pagination -> previous_page and pagination -> next_page respectively.\

The pagination -> next_page alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the next page of results. previous : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the previous page in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key pagination -> previous_page and pagination -> next_page respectively.\

The pagination -> previous_page alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the previous page of results. direction : Sorting order for the results according to the `sort_by` parameter.\

There are two options; `asc` (ascending order) and `desc` (descending order).\

The default is `asc` (ascending). (asc, desc) sort_by : Sort the results by one of the workload parameters. Only one parameter can be selected.\

Allowed options: `resource`, `data_type`,\`last_timestamp\`,\`created\`, `updated`.\

The default is `resource` data: requests.TimeseriesList, optional kwargs: > Extra parameters for requests.TimeseriesList > : - resource: list > - source: list

inner_classes.name: kelvin.sdk.client.config.Configuration.Config
documentation: Basic configuration defaults.

Configuration Base Model.

When creating derived classes, optionally set the following attributes:

- `_FILENAME`: Default filename to use when saving configuration for the first time.
  This can be templated to depend upon environment and `XDG_*` variables.
- `_TEMPLATE`: Optional Jinja2 template to use when creating initial configuration files.
- `_KEY`: Optional key (e.g. `kelvin.client`, with dots corresponding to nested levels)
  to access configuration data if nested within a larger structure.
- `_FACTORY_NAME`: Optional to use for for class factory.
- `_ENV_PREFIX` can be specified (e.g. `KELVIN_CLIENT__`) to map
  environment variables at configuration load-time to override loaded values.

:::{note}
Environment variables \_always\_ override values loaded from configuration (and defaults).
:::

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.AssetType.list_asset_type
documentation:
List Asset Type Returns a filtered dictionary with a data object containing an array of \Kelvin Asset Type\ objects on the Kelvin Platform that meet the criteria of any parameters passed with the API request. If no query parameters are given with the API request, then the defaults will be used to return the \Kelvin Asset Type\ list. The defaults are the first 20 \Kelvin Asset Type\ objects returned in alphabetical order by the unique identifier name. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.asset_type.read`. `listAssetType`: `GET` `/api/v4/assets/types/list` # Parameters names : Filter on the \Kelvin Asset Type\ parameter `name`. This is given as an array, for example `[pump,fan]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a search with `[pump,fan]` will return \Kelvin Asset Type\ with the name `pump` but not with the names `pumps` or `centrifugal-fan`. search : Search and filter the \Kelvin Asset Type\ list. Both the Display Name and the Name will be included in the search field criteria. This is given as an array, for example `[pump,fan]`.\

The search is case insensitive and will find partial matches as well. For example if a Kelvin Asset Type name is `centrifugal_pump`, then a match will be made if the search string is `pum` or `FUGaL`. pagination_type : Method of handling pages in the returned data. There are three options available:\

limits: This is used with the query parameters `page` to select which page of data to return. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data.\

cursor: This is used with the query parameters `next` and `previous` which uses a string as the start or end location reference. The string to use is returned in the previous query json response in the keys `next_page` and `previous_page`.\

stream: This will return all the data in one response. (limits, cursor, stream) page_size : Number of app objects to be returned. Page size can be between 1 and 1000 objects.\

The default is 20 page : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `limits`.\

An integer for the wanted page of results. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data. next : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the next page of apps in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key `pagination -> previous_page` and `pagination -> next_page` respectively.\

The `pagination -> next_page` alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the next page of results. previous : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the previous page of apps in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key `pagination -> previous_page` and `pagination -> next_page` respectively.\

The `pagination -> previous_page` alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the previous page of results. direction : Sorting order for the results according to the `sort_by` parameter.\

There are two options; `asc` (ascending order) and `desc` (descending order).\

The default is `asc` (ascending). (asc, desc) sort_by : Sort the results by one of the asset type parameters. Only one parameter can be selected.\

An `id` must be made only of hexadecimal numbers and the `-` character.\

There are three options available;\

limits: This is used with the query parameters `page` to select which page of data to return. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data.\

cursor: This is used with the query parameters `next` and `previous` which uses a string as the start or end location reference. The string to use is returned in the previous query json response in the keys `next_page` and `previous_page`.\

stream: This will return all the data in one response. (limits, cursor, stream) page_size : Number of items to be returned in each page. Page size can be between 1 and 1000 objects.\

The default is 20 page : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `limits`.\

An integer for the wanted page of results. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data. next : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the next page in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key pagination -> previous_page and pagination -> next_page respectively.\

The pagination -> next_page alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the next page of results. previous : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the previous page in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key pagination -> previous_page and pagination -> next_page respectively.\

The pagination -> previous_page alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the previous page of results. direction : Sorting order for the results according to the `sort_by` parameter.\

There are two options; `asc` (ascending order) and `desc` (descending order).\

Allowed options: `name`, `title`,\`created\`,\`updated\`.\

The default is `name` search : Search recommendation types by name or title

documentation:
Upload Assets Upload a csv (comma-separated values) file with up to 20,000 assets to add as \Kelvin Assets\ into the \Kelvin Platform\. Each row represents one \Kelvin Assets\. The first row of the csv file needs to be the header of the columns. `NAME,DISPLAY NAME,ASSET TYPE`. Details of the options for each column are explained in this \Kelvin Documentation\ link. The file needs to be saved in UTF-8 format with a comma separating each field. Do not use other separation options available in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets such as semi-colons or colons. Strings in the csv file can be with or without quotation marks. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.asset.create`. `uploadAssets`: `POST` `/api/v4/assets/create/upload` # Parameters file : Path and name of the CSV File to upload to the \Kelvin Platform\.\

You can download a blank copy of the template from the \Kelvin Manager UI\. Instructions can be found in the \Kelvin Documentation\.

Both the Display Name and the Name will be included in the search field criteria. This is given as an array, for example `[pump,fan]`.\

The search is case insensitive and will find partial matches as well. For example if a \Kelvin Asset\ name or display name is `centrifugal_pump`, then a match will be made if the search string is `pum` or `FUGaL`. names : Filter on the \Kelvin Asset\ parameter `name`. This is given as an array, for example `[pump,fan]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a search with `[pump,fan]` will return \Kelvin Asset\ with the name `pump` but not with the names `pumps` or `centrifugal-fan`. asset_type_name : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Asset Type\ by its name. This is given as an array, for example `[pump,fan]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a filter with `[pump,fan]` will return \Kelvin Asset Type\ with the name `pump` but not with the names `pumps` or `centrifugal-fan`. status_state : Filter \Kelvin Asset\ by its current status. Multiple parameters can be given in an array, for example `[online,partially_online]` and will be filtered as `OR`.\

There are four parameter options; `online`, `offline`, `partially_online` and `unknown`. pagination_type : Method of handling pages in the returned data. There are three options available:\

limits: This is used with the query parameters `page` to select which page of data to return. cursor: This is used with the query parameters `next` and `previous` which uses a string as the start or end location reference. The string to use is returned in the previous query json response in the keys `next_page` and `previous_page`.\

stream: This will return all the data in one response. (limits, cursor, stream) page_size : Number of app objects to be returned. Page size can be between 1 and 1000 objects.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key `pagination -> previous_page` and `pagination -> next_page` respectively.\

The `pagination -> previous_page` alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the previous page of results. direction : Sorting order for the results according to the `sort_by` parameter.\

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.Asset.update_asset_type
documentation:
Update Asset Type Updates an existing \Kelvin Asset Type\ with any new values passed through the body parameters.\

All body parameters are optional and if not provided will remain unchanged.\

Only the unique identifier name can not be changed. If you need to change this parameter, then you will need to make a new \Kelvin Asset Type\ and relink all \Kelvin Assets\ to the new \Kelvin Asset Type\. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.asset_type.update`. `updateAssetType`: `POST` `/api/v4/assets/types/{asset_type_name}/update` # Parameters asset_type_name : \Kelvin Asset Type\ name to retrieve.\

documentation:
Upload Assets Upload a csv (comma-separated values) file with up to 20,000 assets to add as \Kelvin Assets\ into the \Kelvin Platform\. Each row represents one \Kelvin Assets\. The first row of the csv file needs to be the header of the columns. `NAME,DISPLAY NAME,ASSET TYPE`. Details of the options for each column are explained in this \Kelvin Documentation\ link. The file needs to be saved in UTF-8 format with a comma separating each field. Do not use other separation options available in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets such as semi-colons or colons. Strings in the csv file can be with or without quotation marks. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.asset.create`. `uploadAssets`: `POST` `/api/v4/assets/create/upload` # Parameters file : Path and name of the CSV File to upload to the \Kelvin Platform\.\

You can download a blank copy of the template from the \Kelvin Manager UI\. Instructions can be found in the \Kelvin Documentation\.

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.Bridge.deploy_bridge
documentation: Deploy Bridge App Deploy a \Kelvin Bridge\ to a selected \Kelvin Node\. The Kelvin Bridge will handle all the communications and data transfer between external equipment and the \Kelvin Platform\ using one of four communication protocols; OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus TCP / RTU or Emerson ROC. Each protocol will require a different type of payload structure that defines the exact address specifications for each \Kelvin Assets\ // \Kelvin Metric\ pairs. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.bridge.create`. `deployBridge`: `POST` `/api/v4/bridges/deploy` # Parameters data: requests.BridgeDeploy, optional kwargs: > Extra parameters for requests.BridgeDeploy > : - app_version: str > - cluster_name: str > - name: str > - payload: dict > - protocol: str > - title: str

For example if a \Kelvin Bridge\ name or display name is `extruder-bridge`, then a match will be made if the search string is `ext` or `rIDge`. cluster_name : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\'s cluster_name (This is a \Kelvin Node\). This is given as an array, for example `[demo-node-1,extruder-node]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a filter with `[demo-node-1,extruder-node]` will return \Kelvin Bridge\ with the parameter cluster_name `extruder-node` but not with the names `demo- extruder-node` or `extruder-node-one`. protocol : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `protocol`. This is the communication protocol used by the bridge. This is given as an array, for example `[modbus,mqtt]`.\

There are four options available; `modbus`, `opc-ua`, `mqtt`, `roc`. workload_name : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `workload_name`. This is given as an array, for example `[extruder,tank]`.\

For example a filter with `[extruder,tank]` will return \Kelvin Bridge\ with the parameter workload_name `extruder` but not with the workload_name `demo-extruder-bridge` or `bridge-tank`. status_state : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `status_state`. This is the communication protocol used by the bridge. This is given as an array, for example `[failed,stopped]`.\

documentation:
Delete Bridge application Permanently delete an existing \Kelvin Bridge\. This cannot be undone once the API request has been submitted. \Kelvin Bridges\ can be deleted even if the \Kelvin Node\ is unreachable. In this case, the information will be immediately deleted on the \Kelvin Platform\ and the commands to delete on the \Kelvin Node\ will be sent when it next comes online. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.bridge.delete`. `deleteBridge`: `POST` `/api/v4/bridges/{bridge_name}/delete` # Parameters bridge_name : Unique identifier name for the \Kelvin Bridge\.\

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.BridgeItem.deploy_bridge
documentation: Deploy Bridge App Deploy a \Kelvin Bridge\ to a selected \Kelvin Node\. The Kelvin Bridge will handle all the communications and data transfer between external equipment and the \Kelvin Platform\ using one of four communication protocols; OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus TCP / RTU or Emerson ROC. Each protocol will require a different type of payload structure that defines the exact address specifications for each \Kelvin Assets\ // \Kelvin Metric\ pairs. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.bridge.create`. `deployBridge`: `POST` `/api/v4/bridges/deploy` # Parameters data: requests.BridgeDeploy, optional kwargs: > Extra parameters for requests.BridgeDeploy > : - app_version: str > - cluster_name: str > - name: str > - payload: dict > - protocol: str > - title: str

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.BridgeItem.list_bridge
documentation:
List Bridge Returns a dictionary with a data property containing a list of all \Kelvin Bridges\ on the \Kelvin Platform\. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.bridge.read`. `listBridge`: `GET` `/api/v4/bridges/list` # Parameters names : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ by its name. This is given as an array, for example `[extruder-bridge,tank- bridge]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a filter with `[extruder-bridge,tank-bridge]` will return \Kelvin Bridge\ with the name `extruder-bridge` but not with the names `demo-extruder-bridge` or `tank-bridge-one`. search : Search and filter the \Kelvin Bridge\ list. Both the Display Name and the Name will be included in the search field criteria. This is given as an array, for example `[demo,bridge]`.\

The search is case insensitive and will find partial matches as well. For example if a \Kelvin Bridge\ name or display name is `extruder-bridge`, then a match will be made if the search string is `ext` or `rIDge`. cluster_name : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\'s cluster_name (This is a \Kelvin Node\). This is given as an array, for example `[demo-node-1,extruder-node]`.\

The filter is done on the full name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a filter with `[demo-node-1,extruder-node]` will return \Kelvin Bridge\ with the parameter cluster_name `extruder-node` but not with the names `demo- extruder-node` or `extruder-node-one`. protocol : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `protocol`. This is the communication protocol used by the bridge. This is given as an array, for example `[modbus,mqtt]`.\

There are four options available; `modbus`, `opc-ua`, `mqtt`, `roc`. workload_name : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `workload_name`. This is given as an array, for example `[extruder,tank]`.\

The filter is done on the full workload_name only according to unique identifier name rules (Must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The `.`, `_` and `-` characters are also allowed to separate words instead of a space but not at the beginning or end).\

For example a filter with `[extruder,tank]` will return \Kelvin Bridge\ with the parameter workload_name `extruder` but not with the workload_name `demo-extruder-bridge` or `bridge-tank`. status_state : Filter on the parameter \Kelvin Bridge\ `status_state`. This is the communication protocol used by the bridge. This is given as an array, for example `[failed,stopped]`.\

There are five options available; `running`, `stopped`, `failed`, `deploying`, `unreachable`. pagination_type : Method of handling pages in the returned data. There are three options available:\

limits: This is used with the query parameters `page` to select which page of data to return. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data.\

cursor: This is used with the query parameters `next` and `previous` which uses a string as the start or end location reference. The string to use is returned in the previous query json response in the keys `next_page` and `previous_page`.\

stream: This will return all the data in one response. (limits, cursor, stream) page_size : Number of app objects to be returned. Page size can be between 1 and 1000 objects.\

The default is 20 page : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `limits`.\

An integer for the wanted page of results. The start of the data returned will be calculated using the `page_size` and `page` query parameters for the location to start and the query parameters `sort_by` and `direction` will determine the order of the data. next : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the next page of apps in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key `pagination -> previous_page` and `pagination -> next_page` respectively.\

The `pagination -> next_page` alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the next page of results. previous : Used only with query parameter `pagination_type` set as `cursor`.\

An alphanumeric string to move to the previous page of apps in the list.\

The alphanumeric string which are the bookmarks for the beginning and end of the current list is given in the previous JSON response value under the key `pagination -> previous_page` and `pagination -> next_page` respectively.\

The `pagination -> previous_page` alphanumeric string should be used here to show a list of the previous page of results. direction : Sorting order for the results according to the `sort_by` parameter.\

There are two options; `asc` (ascending order) and `desc` (descending order).\

The default is `asc` (ascending). (asc, desc) sort_by : Sort the results by one of the app parameters. Only one parameter can be selected.\

Allowed options: `name`, `title`, `protocol`, `cluster_name`, `workload_name`, `node_name`, `enabled`, `status_state`, `status_last_seen`, `created`, `updated`.\

The default is `name`

methods.name: kelvin.sdk.client.model.responses.BridgeItem.delete_bridge
documentation:
Delete Bridge application Permanently delete an existing \Kelvin Bridge\. This cannot be undone once the API request has been submitted. \Kelvin Bridges\ can be deleted even if the \Kelvin Node\ is unreachable. documentation:

This will trigger the `actions` for the `control change` in the \Recommendation\ to be created on the \Kelvin Platform\. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.recommendation.update`. `updateAcceptRecommendation`: `POST` `/api/v4/recommendations/{recommendation_id}/accept/update` # Parameters recommendation_id : \Recommendation\ `id`.\

An `id` must be made only of hexadecimal numbers and the `-` character.\

It is a Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) to identify each \Recommendation\.\

It is a 128-bit identifier generated using random or pseudo-random numbers and consists of 32 hexadecimal digits separated by hyphens into five groups of varying lengths: 8-4-4-4-12. data: requests.RecommendationAccept, optional kwargs: > Extra parameters for requests.RecommendationAccept > : - confidence: int > - message: str
